name: SUPERNUS PHARMACEUTICALS, INC.
business: Supernus Pharmaceuticals is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing products for the treatment of central nervous system (CNS) diseases, with approved treatments for ADHD, Parkinson's disease, postpartum depression, epilepsy, migraine, cervical dystonia, and chronic sialorrhea. The company sells its products primarily to pharmaceutical wholesalers, specialty pharmacies, and distributors in the United States. It also collaborates with partners like Biogen for joint commercialization of ZURZUVAE.
namedSuppliers: Britannia (a subsidiary of Stada Arzneimittel AG)

exposures.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing products for the treatment of central nervous system (CNS) diseases.

catalysts.sentence: ONAPGO was approved by the FDA in February 2025.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of expanded label for Qelbree
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, the FDA approved an expanded label update for Qelbree to include new data on the pharmacodynamics and use in breastfeeding mothers.

catalysts.event: EMA and MHRA approval for zuranolone for PPD in Europe and UK
catalysts.sentence: In the third quarter of 2025, Biogen received approval for zuranolone for the treatment of PPD by the European Medicines Agency (EMA) and Medicines Healthcare Regulatory Agency (MHRA) in Europe and the United Kingdom (U.K.) respectively.

catalysts.event: Health Canada authorization for ZURZUVAE for PPD
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, ZURZUVAE received Health Canada Authorization in Canada for treatment indicated for adults with PPD.

catalysts.event: Shionogi approval for zuranolone for MDD in Japan
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, Shionogi received approval for a product containing zuranolone for the treatment of MDD by the Pharmaceuticals and Medical Devices Agency in Japan.

catalysts.event: Initiation of follow-on Phase 2b trial for SPN-820
catalysts.sentence: The Company initiated a follow-on Phase 2b multi-center, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial in approximately 200 adults with MDD.

catalysts.event: Expected initiation of Phase 1 SAD/MAD study for SPN-443
catalysts.sentence: The Company expects to initiate a Phase 1 single-ascending/multiple-ascending dose study in adult healthy volunteers in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Resumption of new patient initiation for ONAPGO after supply constraints
catalysts.sentence: On February 24, 2026, we announced that we have made progress in securing additional product supply of ONAPGO from the current supplier and as a result, has resumed new patient initiation.
In addition, we are working with a second supplier that is expected to begin supplying ONAPGO in 2027.

tamClaims.claim: ADHD affects an estimated 6% to 9% of all school-age children, and an estimated 3% to 5% of adults in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: ADHD affects an estimated 6% to 9% of all school-age children, and an estimated 3% to 5% of adults in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Parkinson's Disease affects 1-2% of individuals 65 years and older.
tamClaims.sentence: PD is the second most common progressive neurodegenerative disorder, affecting 1-2% of individuals 65 years and older.

tamClaims.claim: Migraine affects over 39 million individuals in the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Migraine is estimated to affect over 39 million individuals in the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: 70% to 80% of PD patients experience sialorrhea.
tamClaims.sentence: In adults, PD is the most common cause of sialorrhea, with 70% 80% of PD patients experiencing symptoms.

tamClaims.claim: Cervical dystonia prevalence estimates range from 20 to 4,100 per million individuals.
tamClaims.sentence: Estimates of the prevalence of cervical dystonia vary considerably, from 20 to 4,100 per million individuals.
